weather_line: Clear ridgelines · rubber duck conditions excellent · briefcase glow visible from Diagon Alley (Ministry denies)

stories.headline: The Duck at Heron Shelf Had a Note on It: Investigators Wish It Hadn't
body: The rubber duck recovered from the Jeep's passenger-side mirror at Heron Shelf has been examined by the Creature and Curiosity Desk, the Auror office's Property Crimes sub-division, and one retired tracker who asked not to be named but kept referring to the Bandit as 'a professional.' The duck is regulation yellow, in excellent condition, and — this is the part the investigators wish weren't true — has a small square of parchment tucked beneath its left wing.

The note reads, in a neat hand: 'Nice find. — B.' Nothing further. No seal, no date, no owl registry mark. The tracker stared at it for approximately forty seconds before saying, quietly, 'They knew about the second overhang.' He then declined to elaborate and asked for a biscuit.

Christine, reached for comment, studied the note for a shorter time than she studied the Heron Shelf photograph and asked one question: what vehicle made the tracks on the eastern approach, and when. The investigation is ongoing. The Jeep has said nothing but its mirrors are angled very slightly differently than before.

stories.section: Ministry Watch · Objects of Uncertain Intent
stories.headline: Briefcase Emits a Sound; Ministry Says Sound Is 'Ambient'; Seventeen People Recognise It from Somewhere They Cannot Name
body: The Ministry's luminous briefcase, now gold and resident on Level Two since its unexplained migration Friday night, has begun to produce a sound. The sound is low, steady, and — according to every person who has passed it today — unmistakably familiar in a way that makes them stop walking, look at it, and fail to recall where they've heard it before.

Clerk Whitmore, still recovering his sense of purpose after Thursday's failed opening attempt, described it as 'the sound of knowing exactly where you left something important.' The Ministry's official statement characterises this as 'ambient building resonance, common to Level Two corridors in summer.' The briefcase has no handle markings, no tag, and no visible lock — only the glow, now quite warm, and a faint impression on the lid that the Artefact Office has described, in a memo nobody asked them to write, as 'like a monogram, but not one we recognise.'

stories.headline: Sunday Achieves What the Weekdays Only Aspire To: A Full Report from the Household's Three Departments
body: Sources within the household confirm that Sunday is proceeding correctly. The Adventure Desk reports that Christine and Steve returned from Heron Shelf in good order, that the Jeep has been thanked and forgiven for nothing in particular, and that the second overhang — the one only Christine found — has been added to Steve's map in Christine's handwriting, which he regards as an improvement.

The Home Bureau notes that the biscuit situation, described as stable on Saturday, remains stable, though the stability has the particular quality of a ceasefire rather than a peace. Diplomatic observers cite Lucy.

stories.headline: Dispatch from Abroad: The Foreign Correspondent Files a Sunday Note; Confirms the World Is Larger When She's In It
stories.byline: Filed from somewhere considerably further away than Heron Shelf
body: This paper's foreign correspondent — stationed far enough afield that owls arrive slightly ruffled — filed a brief Sunday dispatch noting that news of the Heron Shelf expedition, the duck, and the note reached him in good order and that he read it twice.

The dispatch contains no breaking intelligence, no operational detail, and no suggestion of imminent return. It contains, in full, the following: 'You found the better bit. Obviously.' It has been pinned to the newsroom noticeboard without further editorial comment.

familiars_corner: Lucy: Has been in the vicinity of the biscuit tin no more than four times today, each of which she describes as 'passing through.' No evidence has been found. There is, notably, less evidence than yesterday.

Ellie: Discovered this morning that the window reflects her own face and has been having what sources describe as 'a lovely time of it.' She waved. The reflection waved back. She found this entirely satisfactory and has returned twice.

Jersey: Located the precise warm square of floor where the afternoon light settles between one and three o'clock, installed himself there before the light arrived, and has declined all offers to relocate. He is, by any measure, exactly where he intended to be.
founding_editor_note: That second overhang would've needed a proper bracket. I'd have had one up in an afternoon, plumb and level. Just saying. — D.

in_brief.sport: Gringotts Vaulting League postponed after judges could not agree whether Christine's Tuesday negotiation counted as a qualifying lift; officials reviewing the footage describe it as 'technically impressive.'
in_brief.forecast: Clear skies over the ridgelines through evening; a light probability of mysterious ducks after dark; Ministry advises against listening too carefully to Level Two.

storyline_updates.title: The Duck Bandit
storyline_updates.summary: The Heron Shelf duck has yielded a note: 'Nice find. — B.' Small, neat hand, no registry mark. A retired tracker confirmed the Bandit knew about Christine's second overhang — meaning they arrived after Christine and Steve did, or were watching. The eastern approach tracks are now a lead. Christine has asked the right question. Day 3 should bring a witness, a vehicle description, or a second duck somewhere unexpected — the Bandit appears to be escalating.

storyline_updates.title: The Glowing Briefcase
storyline_updates.summary: The briefcase is now gold, humming, and resident on Level Two. It has begun emitting a sound that everyone recognises but nobody can place — described as 'the sound of knowing where you left something important.' The Artefact Office has noted what may be a monogram on the lid, unrecognised in any registry. The Ministry continues to explain nothing. Day 4 should bring a partial identification — someone who has seen that monogram before, or a record that almost matches — moving toward a Day 5 reveal of origin or ownership.
